title: Lalitha Mahal
tagline: Lalitha Mahal — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: The Lalitha Mahal, now renamed Lalitha Mahal Palace Hotel, is a luxury hotel located in a former royal residence, and the second largest palace in the southern Indian city of Mysor
overview.description: The Lalitha Mahal, now renamed Lalitha Mahal Palace Hotel, is a luxury hotel located in a former royal residence, and the second largest palace in the southern Indian city of Mysore, Karnataka, after the Mysore Palace. It is located near the Chamundi Hills, east of the city. The palace was built in 1921 by Maharaja Krishnaraja Wodeyar IV for the exclusive stay of the Governor-General of India. Built on a raised ground, the palace was fashioned on the lines of St Paul's Cathedral in London and is one of the imposing structures in Mysore.

topPlaces.name: Mysore Race Course
topPlaces.description: Mysore Race Course is a racing track in Mysore city of Karnataka province, India.

topPlaces.name: Mysore division
topPlaces.description: Mysore division, officially Mysuru division, is an administrative division in the southern Indian state of Karnataka. It is one of four administrative divisions in Karnataka, the others being Bangalore division, Belagavi division, and Kalaburagi division.

topPlaces.name: Adaganahalli
topPlaces.description: Adaganahalli is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Krishnarajanagara taluk of Mysore district.

topPlaces.name: Siddhartha Nagar
topPlaces.description: Siddhartha Nagar is an eastern suburb of Mysore city in Karnataka, a south Indian state.

topPlaces.name: Anjali Ashram, Mysore
topPlaces.description: Anjali Ashram is a Christian retreat in Mysore city in the Karnataka province of India.

topPlaces.name: Mysore Sand Sculpture Museum
topPlaces.description: Mysore Sand Sculpture Museum is the first sand sculpture museum in India, located in Mysore, Karnataka. It was inaugurated in 2014, with 150 sand sculptures on display, on a one-acre land at the base of Chamundi Hills.

topPlaces.name: Nadanahalli
topPlaces.description: Nadanahalli is a small village near Mysore city in Karnataka province of India.

topPlaces.name: Raghavendra Nagar
topPlaces.description: Raghavendra Nagar (Kannada: ರಾಘವೇಂದ್ರ ನಗರ, pronounced [raːɡʱaʋeːn̪d̪ra n̪agara] in IPA) is a suburb of Mysuru in Karnataka which in turn is a state of India.

Nadanahalli is a small village near Mysore city in Karnataka province of India.
Raghavendra Nagar (Kannada: ರಾಘವೇಂದ್ರ ನಗರ, pronounced [raːɡʱaʋeːn̪d̪ra n̪agara] in IPA) is a suburb of Mysuru in Karnataka which in turn is a state of India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Lalitha Mahal?
faq.a: The best time to visit Lalitha Mahal is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Lalitha Mahal?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (9 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (6 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Lalitha Mahal?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Lalitha Mahal at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Lalitha Mahal?
faq.a: Stays in Lalitha Mahal start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Lalitha Mahal?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mysore Race Course, Mysore division, Adaganahalli, Siddhartha Nagar, Anjali Ashram, Mysore.
